title: Milpitas Walmart rooftop-solar fire allegation
cause: Walmart's complaint alleged that faulty connectors in the Tesla-installed PV system caused the fire; the claim was not adjudicated as a final official cause determination.
sourceTitle: Walmart Inc. v. Tesla Inc. complaint
summary: The retailer alleged more than $500,000 in losses from the 2016 rooftop fire; the exact date was not stated publicly.

title: Taipei Water Park solar panel fire
cause: Local media reported the fire could have been sparked by soaring temperatures or a short circuit due to overheating of the 10-year-old panels.
summary: A fire broke out at a solar panel installation at the Taipei Water Park during a heatwave, prompting the evacuation of over 1,200 people. The fire was extinguished within half an hour with no injuries reported.

title: Lakeside Walmart rooftop-solar fire allegation
cause: Walmart's complaint alleged that faulty connectors in the Tesla-installed PV system caused the fire; the claim was not adjudicated as a final official cause determination.
sourceTitle: Walmart Inc. v. Tesla Inc. complaint
summary: The complaint's detailed incident paragraph identifies Lakeside, Colorado, while its opening summary says California. Colorado is geocoded here as probable; the exact date and loss were not stated.

title: Bow Wharf residential roof fire
cause: PV involvement was reported by witnesses; the final ignition source was not publicly established.
summary: A large fire damaged the roof of a new residential development with rooftop PV.

title: Crowan residential PV roof fire
cause: Public reporting identified a roof fire involving solar panels; cause was not stated.
summary: Fire crews responded to a roof fire at a property with a PV installation.

title: Beavercreek Walmart rooftop-solar fire allegation
cause: Walmart's complaint attributed the fire to the rooftop PV system and cited water intrusion through an improperly sealed inverter enclosure and melted hardware; the allegation was not adjudicated.
sourceTitle: Walmart Inc. v. Tesla Inc. complaint
summary: The store was evacuated and closed for eight days; Walmart alleged approximately $784,293 in losses.

title: Denton Walmart rooftop-solar fire allegation
cause: Walmart's complaint alleged that the fire originated in the Tesla PV system and involved an inverter; the allegation was not adjudicated as a final official cause determination.
sourceTitle: Walmart Inc. v. Tesla Inc. complaint
summary: The May 2018 event damaged the store roof; the complaint also documented roof-membrane punctures.

title: Indio Walmart rooftop-solar fire allegation
cause: Walmart's investigation alleged the fire originated within a Tesla-installed module and cited hot spots, grounding, wiring and connector-torque issues; the allegation was not adjudicated.
sourceTitle: Walmart Inc. v. Tesla Inc. complaint
summary: Fire engulfed the roof and extended into the store, prompting evacuation; one firefighter was treated for smoke inhalation and Walmart alleged multimillion-dollar losses.

title: Redlands Amazon rooftop-solar fire
cause: Amazon confirmed that a Tesla solar installation caught fire; no failed component or final public root-cause determination was reported.
summary: A small, contained fire occurred on the fulfillment-center rooftop in June 2018; no injuries were reported.

title: Weeke house solar-array fire
cause: An electrical fault in the PV array was reported as the most likely cause.
summary: A house fire required a prolonged response; the PV array was identified as the most likely origin.

title: Yuba City Walmart rooftop-solar fire allegation
cause: Walmart's complaint alleged that arcing in the de-energized Tesla PV system degraded insulation and incinerated part of the roof; the allegation was not adjudicated.
sourceTitle: Walmart Inc. v. Tesla Inc. complaint
summary: Damage was discovered on November 29 after an event believed to have occurred between June 5 and September 11; Walmart alleged about $125,000 in repair and investigation costs.

title: California Valley Solar Ranch fire
cause: A bird bridged energized conductors, creating sparks that ignited vegetation.
summary: The incident burned more than 1,100 acres and temporarily removed most plant capacity from service.

title: Yamakura Dam floating-PV fire
cause: Typhoon Faxai displaced modules and array sections; fire occurred during the event.
summary: A typhoon damaged the floating array and complicated firefighting at the installation.

title: Humpty Doo commercial PV fire
cause: The public incident account did not identify a final cause.
summary: A commercial rooftop array burned but the fire was contained before spreading through the building.

title: Fresno distribution-center PV fire
cause: Public reports linked the incident to the rooftop PV installation.
summary: Hundreds of rooftop modules were damaged at a distribution facility.

title: Coleridge Community College roof fire
cause: Reporting indicated the sports-hall PV system as a possible origin.
summary: The school sports-hall roof sustained extensive damage.

title: Ullum II solar park inverter fire
cause: A fire occurred in an electrical inverter at the solar park; the company stated they were conducting technical audits to determine the exact cause.
summary: A fire in an inverter at the Ullum II solar park affected 3 MW of the 82 MW capacity and was quickly controlled by volunteer firefighters.

title: Perryville Amazon rooftop-PV fire
cause: The Maryland State Fire Marshal's preliminary finding classified the event as accidental and described an unspecified event involving the solar-panel system.
sourceTitle: Fox News citing Maryland State Fire Marshal
summary: About 60 firefighters controlled the rooftop event in 80 minutes; damage was estimated at $500,000 and one employee was evaluated without a reported injury.

title: Lörrach residential solar panel fire
cause: A solar panel on the roof of a house caught fire slightly and was extinguished and disconnected by the fire brigade.
summary: A minor fire occurred involving a single solar panel on a residential roof in Lörrach, which was quickly extinguished without causing damage to the building itself.

title: Montbonnot gymnasium roof fire
cause: The reported incident involved the building's PV-covered roof; final cause was not stated.
summary: Roughly 70 firefighters responded and approximately half of the building was destroyed.

title: Gunsan solar power plant inverter fire
cause: An investigation by the Korean Fire Protection Association found that moisture condensation and dust accumulation led to insulation breakdown and arc tracking between the anode and cathode plates in the inverter.
summary: A fire occurred in an inverter at a solar power plant in Gunsan, caused by insulation failure due to moisture and dust. No casualties were reported.

title: Queensland residential solar panel isolator fire
cause: A solar panel isolator exploded on a roof, with the homeowner reporting a huge bang and finding the box broken and twisted.
summary: A homeowner in Queensland experienced a solar panel isolator explosion on his roof, prompting calls for increased maintenance checks on older systems.

title: Finley Solar Farm vegetation fire
cause: Cause was under investigation in the public report.
summary: A fire affected about five hectares within the solar-farm site.

title: Newburyport printing-plant rooftop-PV fire
cause: Fire burned within a large rooftop PV array; the fire chief said the cause remained under investigation.
sourceTitle: WCVB reporting from Newburyport fire officials
summary: A three-alarm fire affected the 150-by-60-foot rooftop array; dozens evacuated, no one was injured, and damage was estimated at $2 million to $4 million.

title: Glenn Dale commercial roof PV fire
cause: Officials reportedly believed multiple solar panels were involved in ignition.
summary: Firefighters responded to flames on a commercial roof fitted with PV.

title: Church Crookham flats roof fire
cause: Solar panels were involved; final ignition source was not included in the public account.
summary: The roof of a block of flats was destroyed during the fire.

title: Kappel-Grafenhausen solar park inverter fire
cause: A smoldering fire occurred in the inverter system of a solar park for unknown reasons, causing significant property damage.
summary: A fire broke out in an inverter container at a solar park next to a theme park, resulting in high five-figure property damage but no injuries.

title: Gigean residential roof fire
cause: The roof of a house covered with photovoltaic panels caught fire and collapsed; the solar installation is strongly suspected as the origin, though the exact cause is pending investigation.
summary: A spectacular nighttime fire destroyed the roof of a house in Gigean, causing the structure to collapse and forcing the relocation of the residents.

title: MGM Mega Solar Array transformer arson
cause: According to the arrest report, a man crashed and burned a vehicle next to a transformer; this was an external attack on a PV facility, not a PV equipment failure.
summary: The transformer was damaged and the utility-scale solar facility temporarily shut down; no employees were present and no injuries were reported.

title: Sandisfield residential solar-panel fire
cause: A rooftop solar panel apparently erupted in flames, destroying part of the roof; the cause is undetermined and under investigation by a state electrical inspector.
summary: A stubborn fire involving a rooftop solar panel burned through a residential roof and reignited several times, causing attic damage but no injuries.

title: Falmouth residential solar-panel fire
cause: Cause was not publicly reported.
summary: Firefighters contained a fire in rooftop panels and checked the attic for extension.

title: Essen-Steele apartment building roof fire
cause: A fire broke out under the roof tiles and newly installed solar panels of an apartment building; the cause is unclear and under police investigation.
summary: A fire developed under the roof tiles and solar panels of an apartment building in Essen-Steele, requiring the fire brigade to remove roof coverings to extinguish it.

title: Gosport apartment-block PV fire
cause: A fire involved panels on the roof; final cause was not stated.
summary: Residents were evacuated while crews tackled a rooftop PV fire.

title: Voerde community center flat roof PV fire
cause: A fire broke out on the flat roof of a community center involving solar panels; initial findings suggest a technical defect was the cause.
summary: Approximately 20 square meters of solar panels caught fire on the flat roof of a community center in Voerde, causing an estimated 15,000 euros in damage.

title: Greenford council house roof fire
cause: A London Fire Brigade spokesperson confirmed the blaze involved a solar panel and said the blaze was believed to be accidental.
summary: A fire sparked by solar panels destroyed the roof of a council house in West London, forcing the family to relocate. Firefighters used PVStop to isolate power to other solar panels in the neighbourhood.

title: Antioch Office Depot rooftop-PV fire
cause: Firefighters found multiple solar panels and a small roof area burning; the public report did not establish whether PV equipment initiated the fire.
sourceTitle: Contra Costa News quoting Con Fire
summary: Office Depot and a neighboring PetSmart were evacuated; the fire caused significant water damage and temporarily closed the store.

title: Beaumaris residential solar panel fire
cause: Fire Rescue Victoria stated it is believed the incident may have been sparked by an electrical fault related to a solar panel.
summary: A fire broke out in the back roof of a double-storey home in Hepburn Avenue, Beaumaris, and was quickly controlled by firefighters.

title: Miramar shopping center rooftop PV fire
cause: A small fire occurred in the solar panels located on the roof of the shopping center. The cause was not detailed in the official statement.
summary: A small fire broke out in the rooftop solar panels of the Miramar shopping center. It was quickly controlled by emergency services and the center reopened normally.

title: Douglas County residential solar-panel fire
cause: Authorities reported that the fire involved solar panels, but the exact cause remains under investigation.
summary: An investigation was launched after a fire at a Douglas County home that authorities stated involved solar panels.

title: Mannum Stage 2 solar farm inverter fire
cause: An inverter in a shipping container burst into flames during the commissioning process.
summary: An inverter fire at the Mannum Stage 2 solar farm injured a worker and caused an estimated $167,170 in damages.

title: Araquari industrial solar roof fire
cause: The fire affected approximately 30 square meters of the roof where solar panels were installed; the exact cause was under investigation.
summary: A fire broke out on the roof of an industrial building along BR-101, affecting the solar panel installation and requiring 15,000 liters of water to extinguish.

title: Peterborough logistics-warehouse PV fire
cause: The public engineering account described a fire involving rooftop panels; ignition cause was not stated.
summary: More than 50 firefighters protected a 45,000-square-meter warehouse valued at about £70 million.

title: Poole Travelodge roof fire
cause: The fire service found the blaze in a junction of some solar panels and extinguished it using foam.
summary: Hotel customers were evacuated after a fire involving solar panels broke out on the roof. The fire was extinguished and no issues were found after a reinspection.

title: Sandrigo residential PV fire
cause: Flames reportedly broke out from two photovoltaic panels and quickly spread to the entire ventilated wooden roof.
summary: A severe fire, reportedly starting from photovoltaic panels, destroyed the roof of a renovated courtyard house, rendering it uninhabitable.

title: Zwartsluis residential solar-roof fire
cause: Local reporting described a roof fire apparently originating near the solar installation.
summary: A fire damaged a home roof fitted with PV panels.

title: Sydney Olympic Park Aquatic Centre solar panel fire
cause: Fire and Rescue NSW reported a working fire in the solar panels on the roof of the sporting facility, with investigations underway to determine the cause.
summary: A fire broke out in the solar panels on the roof of the Sydney Olympic Park Aquatic Centre, leading to the safe evacuation of over 2,500 people.

title: Costa del Este bank rooftop PV fire
cause: Local reporting attributed the incident to malfunctioning rooftop PV equipment.
summary: Firefighters extinguished a fire at a bank branch associated with its rooftop solar system.

title: Pembury Estate flats roof fire
cause: Residents and a fire safety expert suspect solar panels were to blame for the blaze, though the official cause is still under investigation by the Metropolitan Police.
summary: A fire gutted the roof of a block of flats in Dalston Lane, damaging 36 homes and forcing the evacuation of about 50 people. A fire risk assessment had failed to declare the presence of solar panels.

title: Santa Maria di Sala residential PV fire
cause: A roof fire was reportedly triggered by some photovoltaic panels, spreading to the wooden attic before being contained.
summary: Firefighters extinguished a roof fire that was triggered by photovoltaic panels and had spread to the wooden attic of a building.

title: San Martino del Lago farm PV fire
cause: A fire involved photovoltaic panels, a generator, and brushwood alongside a farmhouse; the cause is under investigation.
summary: A fire at a farmhouse involved photovoltaic panels and a generator, requiring a massive response from firefighters to contain the blaze.

title: Cumberland industrial building solar-panel fire
cause: Crews found solar panels on fire on the roof; the cause of the fire is under investigation by the state fire marshal.
summary: A fire involving rooftop solar panels broke out at an industrial building, prompting an evacuation and causing heat exhaustion for some firefighters, though there was no structural damage.

title: Kian Teck Road factory rooftop-PV fire
cause: Preliminary investigations by SCDF indicate the fire was likely of electrical origin from the solar panels.
summary: A fire involving rooftop solar panels at a factory in Kian Teck Road led to the evacuation of 76 workers, with no injuries reported.

title: Boyle Heights cold-storage PV fire
cause: LAFD confirmed that panels on the roof were burning; the public incident note did not state the ignition cause.
summary: Firefighters contained burning panels across a nearly 500,000-square-foot roof and saved the building.

title: Osmannoro warehouse roof fire
cause: Public reports noted PV on the burning roof; the final cause was not stated.
summary: Multiple crews responded to a large warehouse-roof fire that produced a heavy smoke plume.

title: Pamplona police-station solar roof fire
cause: Reporting located the origin at the PV-covered roof, with fire spreading through timber supports.
summary: Fire spread through roof supports beneath the photovoltaic installation.

title: Scottsdale Potato Barn rooftop-PV fire
cause: A company representative stated that while the exact cause is unconfirmed, photos and videos suggest it may have been caused by a solar panel malfunction.
summary: A fire broke out on the roof of a furniture store, activating sprinklers inside and causing substantial loss, though no injuries were reported.

title: Brussels office building solar-roof fire
cause: The fire started in the solar panels on the roof of the office building; the exact cause is not yet known.
summary: A fire broke out in the solar panels on the roof of a recently completed seven-story office building, leading to a safe evacuation of all occupants.

title: Alcalde Mayor solar park construction fire
cause: A fire broke out among stored solar panels waiting to be installed; the cause was unknown at the time of reporting.
summary: A small fire affected a group of stored solar panels at the Alcalde Mayor solar park construction site, causing only material damage.

title: Barneveld warehouse solar-roof fire
cause: A PV short circuit was publicly cited as the suspected cause.
summary: The fire was controlled quickly, but smoke, soot and inspection impacts were reported.

title: Raywood solar farm transformer fire
cause: A transformer caught fire on a moderately sized commercial solar farm, which is under investigation by Energy Safe Victoria.
summary: A transformer fire occurred at a solar farm near Raywood, Victoria, producing toxic smoke but no threat to the community.

title: Peoria solar farm fire
cause: A department investigator was unable to determine the cause and origin of the fire, which burned grass under the panel.
summary: A fire at a solar panel farm off North Galena Road caused $1,000 in damage to a panel and burned surrounding grass.

title: Norden warehouse photovoltaic fire
cause: Cause was not established in the cited public account.
summary: A warehouse PV fire triggered a large emergency response and roof-opening work.

title: East Windsor solar-facility transformer fire
cause: An exploding transformer ignited dry vegetation at the solar facility.
summary: Fire affected a roughly 100-by-50-foot area but crews prevented spread to solar panels and nearby structures.

title: Heilbronn industrial-roof module fire
cause: Several modules burned; final ignition cause was not stated.
summary: Crews opened bitumen roofing and removed EPS insulation to extinguish smoldering material.

title: Laas fruit-warehouse PV-zone fire
cause: Public reporting identified a suspected technical origin in the PV zone.
summary: A roof fire affected roofing and insulation at a fruit cooperative warehouse.

title: Halle school solar-roof fire
cause: The fire brigade suspects that a technical defect in one of the solar panels caused the fire.
summary: A fire destroyed about 100 solar panels on the roof of a school, causing a large smoke plume but no injuries as few students were present.

title: Pasadena warehouse rooftop-PV fire
cause: Authorities reported workers were doing maintenance or installation work when the solar panels suddenly caught fire, though the fire department has not confirmed the exact cause.
summary: A fire started on the roof of a Lineage Company warehouse in Pasadena, charring the solar panels but causing no reported injuries.

title: Apeldoorn commercial solar-roof fire
cause: A fire broke out involving approximately 25 solar panels on the roof of a commercial building; the cause is still under investigation.
summary: A fire involving about 25 solar panels occurred on the roof of a commercial building in Apeldoorn, which was quickly controlled by the fire brigade.

title: St Michael's Hospital roof fire
cause: Avon Fire and Rescue Service confirmed the cause was accidental and due to a fault in the solar panels.
summary: A blaze began in solar panels on the roof of a maternity hospital, forcing the evacuation of pregnant women and babies. The fire was safely extinguished with no injuries reported.

title: Tzrufa agricultural building solar roof fire
cause: A fire broke out in the solar panels on the roof of an agricultural building; firefighters disconnected the power and extinguished it before it spread.
summary: Firefighters from the Hadera station extinguished a fire involving solar panels on an agricultural building's roof in Moshav Tzrufa, preventing extensive damage.

title: Lona Lases residential PV fire
cause: The fire started in the photovoltaic panels of a house located above a bar near a gas station; the origin is under investigation.
summary: A fire involved the photovoltaic panels of a residence situated near a gas station, but firefighters prevented the flames from spreading to the rest of the building.

title: Bochum-Hiltrop residential rooftop-PV fire
cause: A fire broke out on the roof of a single-family home where a PV system was installed; the fire was quickly extinguished and the cause is under investigation by the criminal police.
summary: A fire occurred on the roof of a single-family home involving a PV system, which was quickly extinguished by the fire brigade from the ground.

title: Rotterdam residential solar-roof fire
cause: A large fire occurred on the roof of a residential building, which also involved the solar panels; the cause is being investigated by the fire brigade.
summary: A major roof fire spread to an adjacent home and involved solar panels, prompting the evacuation of the residential block.

title: Bochum-Wattenscheid balcony solar fire
cause: Parts of a balcony solar power plant caught fire and developed strong heat and smoke; the police have started investigating the cause.
summary: A fire broke out involving a balcony solar power plant on the first floor of a building, causing smoke damage to the apartment and requiring two residents to be treated by emergency services.

title: Shanklea Primary School roof fire
cause: Solar panels on the roof were ablaze, prompting the council to isolate solar panels across similar properties while the matter is investigated.
summary: A fire broke out at Shanklea Primary School, leading to the disconnection of solar panels at over 80 other schools. The council had been aware of potential safety issues with the panels for over a year.

title: Fleurance middle school roof fire
cause: A fire broke out on the roof involving photovoltaic panels, following a similar incident the day before; the exact cause is under investigation, but the department ordered the immediate removal of the panels.
summary: A fire destroyed 500 square meters of roofing at the Hubert Reeves middle school in Fleurance, involving the building's solar panels.

title: Casarsa della Delizia residential PV fire
cause: The fire involved the photovoltaic panels on the roof of a condominium, but the exact causes are still under investigation.
sourceTitle: Vigili del Fuoco Friuli Venezia Giulia
summary: A fire broke out involving the photovoltaic panels on the roof of a condominium, which was quickly extinguished by the fire brigade with no injuries reported.

title: Centro Comercial Colombo rooftop PV fire
cause: A short circuit in an electrical panel connected to a photovoltaic panel on the roof caused the fire, according to firefighters and the shopping center management.
summary: A short circuit in a rooftop solar panel installation caused a small fire at the Colombo shopping center in Lisbon. The fire was quickly extinguished by firefighters with only material damage.

title: Montebelluna residential PV fire
cause: The photovoltaic system on the roof of a private home caught fire, with the cause not explicitly stated.
summary: Firefighters intervened to extinguish a fire involving a rooftop photovoltaic system on a private residence, preventing the flames from spreading.

title: Villalba de Guardo solar farm fire
cause: Fire affected the solar panels at the facility, requiring intervention by firefighters and environmental agents to prevent spread to the surrounding field. The exact cause was not officially determined.
summary: A fire broke out at the Villalba de Guardo photovoltaic plant, affecting the solar panels. Firefighters extinguished the blaze before it could spread to the surrounding countryside.

title: Gallarate residential PV fire
cause: The solar panels on the roof of a recently renovated villa caught fire; the cause of the ignition is under investigation.
summary: A fire broke out involving the solar panels on the roof of a villa, requiring a lengthy intervention by firefighters to secure the area.

title: Reston residential rooftop-PV fire
cause: Fairfax County Fire and Rescue determined that an electrical event involving the rooftop solar panels caused the fire.
summary: The fire was isolated to exterior roof panels; no one was injured, the family reoccupied the home, and damage was estimated at $60,527.

title: Beryl solar-farm fire
cause: Cause was not stated in the indexed incident report.
summary: Crews contained an approximately 10-acre fire threatening structures at the solar farm.

title: Viddalba solar farm arson
cause: Two men allegedly set fire to a solar park under construction, destroying 5,000 panels and a forklift.
summary: An arson attack at a solar farm under construction destroyed 5,000 photovoltaic panels and equipment.

title: Norwalk auto-dealership PV fire
cause: The fire marshal was investigating the cause at publication.
summary: Multiple rooftop panels burned; crews extinguished the incident in roughly ten minutes.

title: Carmi solar farm field fire
cause: A field fire occurred at the solar farm, limited to vegetation with no damage to the solar panels.
summary: Firefighters battled a vegetation fire at an Ameren Missouri solar farm north of Carmi; no panels were damaged.

title: Lunwei East District floating solar park cable fire
cause: The fire was reportedly caused by a short circuit in a cable, resulting in the combustion of around 30 meters of cable line.
summary: A cable fire broke out at the 192 MW Lunwei East District floating solar park, damaging 30 meters of cable but causing no injuries.

title: Wellington North Solar Farm grass fire
cause: A grass fire broke out at the site during heatwave conditions; the cause is still being assessed.
summary: A 92-hectare grass fire occurred at the 400 MW Wellington North Solar Farm, causing limited damage to the northeast corner of the site.

title: Meißenheim half-timbered house PV fire
cause: After ruling out arson, investigators suspect a technical defect in the PV system's inverter caused the fire.
summary: A major fire destroyed a half-timbered house, with investigations pointing to a technical defect in the PV system's inverter as the likely cause, resulting in approximately 800,000 euros in damage.

title: Northampton County solar farm wildfire
cause: Officials suspect the fire originated from unauthorized activity, potentially wire theft, after a hole was found cut into the facility's fence.
summary: A wildfire broke out at a solar installation and spread into surrounding woods, burning approximately 45 acres.

title: Kankakee County solar farm fire
cause: A fire involved an active panel within the facility following severe storms and tornadoes in the region.
summary: Multiple fire agencies responded to a fire involving an active panel at a rural solar farm southwest of Kankakee amidst tornado recovery efforts.

title: IES Manacor high school rooftop PV fire
cause: The fire was located in an electrical panel linked to the rooftop solar panel installation. The origin was described as strictly technical.
summary: A fire in an electrical panel connected to the rooftop solar installation forced the evacuation of the IES Manacor high school. Firefighters disconnected the PV system and extinguished the fire without structural damage or injuries.

title: Pingtung Linbian solar panel storage fire
cause: The fire occurred at a temporary storage yard for solar panels; the exact cause is under investigation, with authorities noting it could be due to improper storage leading to spontaneous combustion.
summary: A fire at a solar panel temporary storage yard in Linbian burned for about 4 hours, covering an area of 8,000 square meters. No casualties were reported, but the smoke affected nearby villages.

title: Olney residential rooftop-PV fire
cause: Montgomery County Fire and Rescue reported fire confined to the rooftop solar panels; a final ignition cause was not stated.
summary: Crews prevented extension into the house and used PVStop to isolate the array; there were no injuries and the family reoccupied the home.

title: Champaign residential rooftop-PV fire
cause: The fire department determined the fire was happening on a solar energy system panel and deemed it accidental in nature.
summary: Firefighters quickly extinguished an accidental fire on a residential solar panel system, preventing smoke or damage inside the home.

title: West Jefferson fulfillment-center PV fire
cause: Cause was not established in initial reporting.
summary: Approximately 75–100 rooftop panels burned and the facility was evacuated.

title: Middleton Operations Center rooftop-PV fire
cause: The fire chief suspected an electrical issue involving the solar system; the final cause remained under investigation.
summary: Seven panels, electrical equipment and roof structure were damaged; crews extinguished the fire in about ten minutes and no one was injured.

title: Toms River residential solar-system fire
cause: The Bureau of Fire Prevention determined that an accidental electrical failure in the rooftop solar-panel area started the fire.
sourceTitle: Jersey Shore Online citing fire investigators
summary: The two-story home was declared unsafe and uninhabitable; no injury was reported.

title: Emmerthal commercial hall roof PV fire
cause: A fire broke out in the area of a solar system installed on the roof of a commercial hall; initial findings point to a technical defect.
summary: A fire occurred on the roof of a commercial hall in Emmerthal, likely caused by a technical defect in the solar system, resulting in approximately 10,000 euros in damage.

title: Hainesville residential PV-system roof fire
cause: The fire marshal reported that energized electrical activity, arcing and failure in the rooftop PV system caused the fire.
sourceTitle: Lake & McHenry County Scanner citing fire marshal
summary: Fire spread from the solar installation into the roof and left the home uninhabitable; no injuries were reported.

title: Den Bosch residential solar-roof fire
cause: The fire originated near the solar panels integrated into the roof; the exact cause is under investigation.
summary: A fire broke out on the roof of two homes involving integrated solar panels, leading to the precautionary evacuation of seven homes.

title: Stratford residential solar-panel fire
cause: The local fire marshal was investigating the ignition source.
summary: Multiple rooftop panels burned with extension into roofing material; occupants evacuated safely.

title: Vialonga DHL warehouse rooftop PV fire
cause: The fire started on the roof of the warehouse, with firefighters suspecting problems with the photovoltaic panels, similar to a previous fire at the same location weeks earlier. Official investigation is pending.
summary: A major fire completely destroyed a DHL logistics warehouse in Vialonga, starting on the roof where solar panels were installed. This was the second fire at the facility in less than a month, raising suspicions about the PV system.

title: Sint-Katelijne-Waver commercial solar-roof fire
cause: Initial reports indicate the fire was caused by a short circuit in the solar panels on the roof of the commercial hall.
summary: A severe fire broke out on the roof of a commercial building, producing a thick black smoke plume, suspected to be caused by a short circuit in the solar panels.

title: Seguin bottling-plant rooftop-PV fire
cause: Solar panels on the approximately one-million-square-foot plant roof burned; the ignition cause remained under investigation.
summary: The plant was evacuated; panels and roof were heavily damaged, light smoke reached the interior, and no injuries were reported.

title: Salteras solar farm inverter fire
cause: The fire affected one of the inverters in a field of photovoltaic panels. The exact cause of the inverter failure was not detailed.
summary: Firefighters from Santiponce responded to a fire involving an inverter at a solar farm in Salteras. The incident resulted only in material damage with no injuries reported.

title: Uberlândia residential solar roof fire
cause: The homeowner alleged that the fire started in the solar panel system, but the official cause has not been determined.
summary: A fire destroyed part of a house in the Shopping Park neighborhood; the owner suspected the solar panels were the origin.

title: Meinier residential solar panel fire
cause: Solar panels on the roof of a building caught fire; the exact cause was not immediately reported.
summary: A significant roof fire involving solar panels occurred on a building in the center of Meinier, requiring a large firefighter response.

title: Pattani solar farm arson attack
cause: Assailants broke into a solar power plant construction site and set fire to a solar panel storage area.
summary: An arson attack at a solar power plant construction site in Pattani damaged a solar panel storage area, with no injuries reported.

title: Asheville Chamber of Commerce rooftop-PV fire
cause: A fire was contained to a single solar panel on the roof of the building; the cause was not specified.
summary: A small fire broke out on a single solar panel atop the Asheville Chamber of Commerce building and was quickly extinguished with no reported damages.

title: Boyle Heights cold-storage warehouse fire
cause: The facility operator said the fire began while contractors were testing rooftop solar panels; the official investigation remained open.
summary: A multi-day warehouse fire prompted shelter-in-place actions and was complicated by an ammonia release.

title: Sidegate Primary School roof fire
cause: Firefighters confirmed beyond reasonable doubt that the cause was a solar panel on the roof, though the exact electrical fault remains under investigation.
summary: A fire broke out on the roof of Sidegate Primary School, leading to the evacuation of 650 pupils and 100 staff. The incident prompted the council to temporarily switch off solar panels at 80 schools.

title: La Mudarra solar farm fire
cause: A fire broke out in a solar farm with 228,000 panels. Firefighters reportedly mentioned possible ground faults or reflections as potential hazards, but no official cause was confirmed.
summary: A fire occurred at a large solar farm in La Mudarra, prompting intervention by firefighters. The incident sparked debate among local farmers regarding fire prevention regulations for solar installations.

title: Amarillo apartment complex rooftop-PV fire
cause: The Amarillo Fire Marshal ruled the fire accidental, but the official cause could not be determined; the fire involved the rooftop solar panel system.
summary: A fire involving rooftop solar panels displaced 10 residents and caused moderate damage to the building, though it did not spread into occupied living spaces.

title: Manchester Walmart rooftop-PV fire
cause: Manchester fire officials reported several banks of rooftop solar panels actively burning; a final ignition cause was not reported.
sourceTitle: CT Insider citing Manchester Fire Department
summary: Firefighters extinguished burning rooftop PV equipment, secured the solar system and checked the roof and store for extension. The roof sustained minor damage, the store closed for about an hour and no injuries were reported.
